name: ingest-project
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /ren:ingest-project. Each test runs a simulated invocation against a fixture and verifies the assertions hold. Load-bearing: the user's project is never modified; writes are additive; no content is invented.

tests.expected_output_summary: Scanner reads the python project read-only; the skill drafts a populated ADR-014 sub-wiki, previews once, and on approval writes it + registers it in the master wiki.
binary_assertions: scripts/scan.py is invoked read-only against the project path before any write
Target directory wiki/projects/demo-api/ exists after the run
All 7 top-level files exist: PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, CONTEXT.md, index.md, log.md
All 3 subdirectories exist with .gitkeep markers: research/, decisions/, patterns/
PROJECT.md frontmatter has type: project-main and status: ingested
REQUIREMENTS.md frontmatter has type: project-requirements
ROADMAP.md frontmatter has type: project-roadmap
STATE.md frontmatter has type: project-state
CONTEXT.md frontmatter has type: project-context
index.md frontmatter has type: project-index
log.md frontmatter has type: project-log-entry
PROJECT.md names the detected stack (Python / FastAPI) drawn from the scanner facts, not invented
log.md contains at least one '## [<YYYY-MM>] backfill |' entry derived from the git timeline
log.md contains a '## [2026-05-31] init | Project sub-wiki ingested for demo-api' entry (simulated_date from fixture)
After the run, wiki/index.md contains exactly ONE bullet under '## Projects' linking to projects/demo-api/index.md (re-running adds no second — idempotent)
After the run, wiki/log.md contains exactly ONE 'init | Project sub-wiki ingested for demo-api' entry (idempotent)
Files written = 7 pages + 3 .gitkeep = 10; master wiki/index.md and wiki/log.md are edits, not new files
No file inside /home/tester/Dev/demo-api is created, modified, or deleted by the run (read-only invariant on the project)
A single approval gate is shown before any wiki file is written

tests.expected_output_summary: Sub-wiki already partially exists → additive-only: only missing pages are written, existing pages are never overwritten, master-wiki registration lines are idempotent.
binary_assertions: Skill output mentions 'already exists' OR 'additive' in the user-facing message
Pre-existing pages PROJECT.md, index.md, log.md under wiki/projects/demo-api/ have unchanged modification times
Pre-existing .gitkeep files under research/, decisions/, patterns/ have unchanged modification times
Missing pages REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, CONTEXT.md ARE written
After the run, wiki/index.md does NOT gain a second projects/demo-api/ bullet (idempotent registration)
After the run, wiki/log.md does NOT gain a second 'init | Project sub-wiki ingested for demo-api' entry (idempotent)
No file inside /home/tester/Dev/demo-api is modified

tests.expected_output_summary: When the master wiki is absent, the skill activates then refuses before scanning and recommends /ren:install. (Override the fixture's wiki to absent when running this case.)
binary_assertions: If wiki/index.md does NOT exist, the skill refuses before invoking scripts/scan.py
The refusal message names '/ren:install' as the remediation
No partial sub-wiki is created at wiki/projects/demo-api/

tests.expected_output_summary: With light depth and a sparse README, sections lacking evidence keep placeholder markers rather than fabricated content.
binary_assertions: REQUIREMENTS.md sections with no supporting evidence contain a '_TBD_' placeholder marker, not invented requirements
No fabricated feature, metric, date, or user-persona appears in any page that is not traceable to the scanner facts
PROJECT.md still records the factually-detected stack (Python / FastAPI)

notes: fixture: path to a YAML file under eval/fixtures/ describing the simulated environment.
trigger_test: when true, the eval harness also verifies SKILL.md's description activates on the prompt (ADR-012 Layer 1).

ingest-no-master-wiki-refused is trigger_test: true because the prompt is a valid /ren:ingest-project invocation that genuinely activates the skill; the refusal is post-activation Stage-1 behavior, not non-activation. Non-activation belongs in non_triggers.
All assertions are binary. The load-bearing ones: project dir unchanged (read-only invariant), writes additive-only, no content invented.

Date-robustness: the log-line date assertion matches '2026-05-31' because the fixture pins simulated_date: 2026-05-31. For other dates use date-agnostic substring 'init | Project sub-wiki ingested for demo-api'.
--depth deep may add decisions/ and research/ summary files; those are outside the v1 binary-eval scope and not asserted here.
